title: Roth IRA Contributions and Recharacterizations Guide

steps.title: Determine Eligibility
steps.description: Confirm your modified adjusted gross income (MAGI) falls within IRS limits for Roth IRA contributions. For 2024, single filers must have MAGI under $161,000 and married filers under $214,000.

steps.title: Calculate Contribution Limit
steps.description: For 2024, the maximum annual contribution is $7,000 ($8,000 if age 50+). Contributions phase out gradually if MAGI is near limits.

steps.title: Gather Required Documents
steps.description: Collect W-2s, 1099s, and previous IRA statements. You'll need proof of income and existing retirement accounts.

steps.title: Submit Contribution
steps.description: Work with your custodian to make a non-deductible contribution. Ensure funds are properly allocated to the Roth IRA.

steps.title: Recharacterization Consideration
steps.description: If you over-contributed or changed retirement plans, you may recharacterize contributions to a Traditional IRA within 8 months of the tax year's end.

steps.title: File IRS Form 8606
steps.description: Report non-deductible contributions and recharacterizations on Form 8606 to track basis for future tax-free withdrawals.
